author: claude
content: DATA-FAILURE POLICY DECIDED (Stefan 2026-06-20): a record that trips the schema (CONSTRAINT VIOLATION) -> write a CLEAR, investigable error to the log (visit_id, table, the constraint, enough context to investigate) AND mark the VISIT as FAILED (incomplete). Do NOT drop-and-continue: a constraint violation means a page found a way to trip our schema = needs MANUAL INVESTIGATION, and the best way to raise it is repeated site failure + a good error message. Distinct from TRANSIENT storage errors (infra blip -> retry per the #1197 forward-progress fix). NON-NEGOTIABLES regardless: never strand (visit reaches terminal=failed), never hang (callback CommandSequence must not block forever), never let one bad record break the SHARED DataSocket (G1b isolation), never SILENTLY drop. This policy resolves adversarial G1/G1b/G2 and aligns #1161 (SQLAlchemy store_record rollback -> roll back + fail-visit-loud, not silent) and #1174 (surface the real finalize error, don't mask as timeout).
